prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetie, let me try to explain this CPU thing you're asking about. It's like the brain of your computer—it does all the thinking and makes decisions about what happens when you click those buttons and type those letters. When I was young, we didn't have anything nearly this clever—just typewriters and rotary phones! This little chip does math problems and follows instructions faster than any person could, millions of times each second. Your grandmother may not understand all the technical details, but I know it's what makes your computer smart enough to play those games you love and help with your homework.
